statement: Go long 2-4 days before earnings only when insider dip-buying and sustained dark-pool accumulation align with bullish short-dated call flow, then exit before print.
rationale: Insider open-market buys and dark-pool accumulation both signal informed positioning. Adding unusual bullish options flow narrows to high-conviction windows where pre-earnings drift is more likely to persist.
entry_trigger: At T-4 to T-1 trading days before earnings, enter long only if all are true: (1) >=1 CEO/CFO open-market buy in prior 30 days and close is within 10-day lower quartile, (2) dark-pool net buy ratio >= 1.25 for at least 3 consecutive sessions, (3) same-week call premium sweep >= $500k and call/put volume ratio >= 2.5x 20-day baseline, (4) stock closes above 5-day VWAP.
exit_trigger: Exit at earliest of +4.0% take-profit, -2.0% stop-loss, options-flow reversal, or close before earnings release date.

risk_controls.event_risk: no overnight hold through earnings print
